gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/336)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/336)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ كَفَّارَةِ الْيَمِينِ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مَا حَدُّ مَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ وَ إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ يَسْأَلُ فِي كَفِّهِ وَ هُوَ يَجِدُ فَقَالَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ عِنْدَهُ فَضْلٌ عَنْ قُوتِ عِيَالِهِ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ لَا يَجِدُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Safwan Bin yahya, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ibrahimasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about an expiation for the oath in the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [5:89] but whosoever cannot find (means) then fasting for three days. What is the limit of the one who does not find, and if the man were to be asked with regarding to what is in his hand, and he finds it’. So heasws said: ‘When there does not happen to be with him any excess from the livelihood of his family, so he is from the ones ‘who cannot find’’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Safwan ibn Yahya from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the expiation for oath mentioned in the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, ‘. . . if one cannot find then it is three days fasting.’ [5:91] and about the limit of ‘if one cannot find’ and that a man asks for help (beggar) but he is not of ‘if one cannot find’. He (the Imam) said, ‘If one does not have more than what his dependents need, then “if one does not find” applies to him.’”
